theater_description: Security-operation zone between Taungpyoletwa and Maungdaw in central/northern Maungdaw Township after the 9 October 2016 attacks on border police outposts.
geometry_method_note: Use the central-to-northern Maungdaw lockdown/security-operation corridor described by OHCHR and ICC; exact village polygon remains unresolved.
military_area_note: Tatmadaw and other security forces operating in the Maungdaw operation zone; do not use the whole Myanmar military.
civilian_area_note: Rohingya villages in the locked-down Maungdaw operation zone; later refugees and IDPs indicate the exposed footprint was far smaller than all of Rakhine.
split_reason: Violence mode and geography differ from both the later two-week November sweep and the much larger 2017 three-township campaign.
quality_note: Civilian deaths and dynamic population exposure are only weakly paired for this sub-window.

theater_description: About ten Rohingya villages in northwest Rakhine / northwest Maungdaw swept by the army after the 12 November 2016 attacks.
geometry_method_note: Build from the Reuters village reconstruction plus OHCHR list of the most affected north-Maungdaw villages; geometry probably follows a village-cluster corridor rather than all of Maungdaw Township.
military_area_note: Reuters reports just under 2,000 soldiers in the area; theater denominator should be limited to units used in the sweep.
civilian_area_note: Use only the Rohingya village cluster that was swept and depopulated, not the whole township or all of Rakhine.
split_reason: This sub-episode has the clearest 2016 civilian-death reporting and a distinct two-week theater.
quality_note: Promising candidate for a later full estimate, but present source base still leaves the exposure denominator too model-heavy.

theater_description: Residual detention, violence, and continuing outward displacement after the late-November 2016 sweeps until the government-declared end of operations in February 2017.
geometry_method_note: Same northern Maungdaw corridor as the 2016 wave, but with rapidly shrinking civilian presence as people fled or hid.
military_area_note: Security-force deployment likely persisted in the same operation zone, but no month-by-month strength series was located.
civilian_area_note: Civilian presence dropped sharply because the zone was partially emptied by forced displacement.
split_reason: The mortality signal becomes harder to disentangle from detention, deprivation, and staggered displacement after the November sweeps.
quality_note: Mostly contextual evidence; direct-death numerators and denominators are not tightly matched.

theater_description: The northern Rakhine three-township belt (Maungdaw, Buthidaung, Rathedaung), especially Rohingya hamlets and wards targeted during and immediately after the 25 August 2017 ARSA attacks.
geometry_method_note: Use the union of Rohingya hamlets and urban wards represented in the hamlet-leader survey across Maungdaw, Buthidaung, and Rathedaung, with dynamic civilian presence reduced by refugee outflow between 25 August and 24 September.
military_area_note: Limit military exposure to state forces directly involved in the three-township campaign; core strict estimate does not use whole-country Tatmadaw strength.
civilian_area_note: Core civilian denominator is Rohingya civilian exposure in the targeted three-township belt, because the best direct-death series is Rohingya-specific; separate Hindu civilian deaths by ARSA are preserved as auxiliary rows rather than blended.
split_reason: This is the best-covered episode, with a tight one-month death floor, displacement checkpoints, and a source-grounded deployment estimate.
quality_note: Strong civilian numerator and denominator triangulation, but combatant-status ambiguity for alleged ARSA dead remains substantial.

theater_description: Late-2017 period of continued expulsions, village destruction, and shrinking Rohingya presence after the first month.
geometry_method_note: Same three-township belt, but geometry contracts sharply as villages are emptied and destroyed.
military_area_note: Units remained active in the same theater, but a month-by-month assignment series was not identified here.
civilian_area_note: Population exposure changes too rapidly after late September to support a robust person-month denominator without more granular flow data.
split_reason: The source base separates the acute first-month mortality floor from the broader late-2017 depopulation/destruction phase.
quality_note: Good contextual evidence, but no comparably tight direct-death numerator for the residual phase.
